Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court, called the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara 
Zimmerman Duterte to order.

blocks.source_text:
The Sergeant-at-Arms, PMGen Alfred S. Corpus, commanded all persons, on pain of penalty, 
to keep silent while the Senate is sitting for the trial of the Articles of Impeachment against Vice 
President Sara Zimmerman Duterte.

blocks.normalized_text: go led the prayer to wit

blocks.source_text: Sen. Christopher Lawrence T. Go led the prayer, to wit:

blocks.normalized_text: almighty and loving god o allah we come before you today with humility mindful of the solemn duty entrusted to us and the weight of the decisions that lie ahead grant us wisdom to discern what is right courage to uphold the truth and fairness in every judgment we make keep us from prejudice anger personal interest and political pressure help us listen with patience speak with honesty and act according to conscience the constitution and the welfare of the filipino people
blocks.raw_text: Almighty and loving God, O Allah, we come before You today with humility, mindful of the solemn duty entrusted to us and the weight of the decisions that lie ahead. Grant us wisdom to discern what is right, courage to uphold the truth, and fairness in every judgment we make. Keep us from prejudice, anger, personal interest, and political pressure. Help us listen with patience, speak with honesty, and act according to conscience, the Constitution, and the welfare of the Filipino people.
blocks.source_text:
Almighty and loving God, O Allah, we come before You today with humility, mindful 
of the solemn duty entrusted to us and the weight of the decisions that lie ahead. Grant 
us wisdom to discern what is right, courage to uphold the truth, and fairness in every 
judgment we make. Keep us from prejudice, anger, personal interest, and political 
pressure. Help us listen with patience, speak with honesty, and act according to 
conscience, the Constitution, and the welfare of the Filipino people.

blocks.normalized_text: gabayan ninyo kaming lahat ng may tungkulin sa mga pagdinig na ito nawa ay ang bawat panig ay mabigyan ng pagkakataong marinig ang bawat ebidensya ay mapag-aralang mabuti at ang bawat pasya ay nakabatay sa katotohanan katarungan at tamang proseso
blocks.raw_text: Gabayan Ninyo kaming lahat ng may tungkulin sa mga pagdinig na ito. Nawa ay ang bawat panig ay mabigyan ng pagkakataong marinig, ang bawat ebidensya ay mapag-aralang mabuti, at ang bawat pasya ay nakabatay sa katotohanan, katarungan, at tamang proseso.
blocks.source_text:
Gabayan Ninyo kaming lahat ng may tungkulin sa mga pagdinig na ito. Nawa 
ay ang bawat panig ay mabigyan ng pagkakataong marinig, ang bawat ebidensya 
ay mapag-aralang mabuti, at ang bawat pasya ay nakabatay sa katotohanan, 
katarungan, at tamang proseso.

blocks.normalized_text: remind us that public office is a sacred trust may we never lose sight of the people whose lives are affected by our words our actions and our decisions let the pursuit of truth strengthen our institutions and deepen our commitment to serve with integrity and compassion

Remind us that public office is a sacred trust. May we never lose sight of the people 
whose lives are affected by our words, our actions, and our decisions. Let the pursuit of 
truth strengthen our institutions and deepen our commitment to serve with integrity and 
compassion.

blocks.normalized_text: lord bring healing to our nation where there is division grant us understanding patience and unity where there is distrust restore faith where there is fear and anger give us calmness and restraint

blocks.source_text:
Lord, bring healing to our nation. Where there is division, grant us understanding, 
patience, and unity. Where there is distrust, restore faith. Where there is fear and anger, 
give us calmness and restraint.

blocks.normalized_text: when these proceedings are concluded help us move forward together nawa ay makabalik na kami sa aming pangunahing tungkulin ang magserbisyo at maglingkod sa ating mga kababayan nawa ay maituon naming muli ang aming buong pansin sa paglilingkod sa ating mga kababayan lalo na sa mga higit na nangangailangan
blocks.raw_text: When these proceedings are concluded, help us move forward together. Nawa ay makabalik na kami sa aming pangunahing tungkulin: ang magserbisyo at maglingkod sa ating mga kababayan. Nawa ay maituon naming muli ang aming buong pansin sa paglilingkod sa ating mga kababayan, lalo na sa mga higit na nangangailangan.
blocks.source_text:
When these proceedings are concluded, help us move forward together. Nawa ay 
makabalik na kami sa aming pangunahing tungkulin: ang magserbisyo at 
maglingkod sa ating mga kababayan. Nawa ay maituon naming muli ang aming 
buong pansin sa paglilingkod sa ating mga kababayan, lalo na sa mga higit na

blocks.normalized_text: lastly may the philippine senate remain united in fulfilling its role as the last bastion of democracy within these halls may truth prevail and the true voice of the filipinos be heard may compassion for others be a steadfast virtue we live by in our words and in our actions and may we your senator-judges be instruments of your grace in the pursuit of peace justice and the common good
blocks.raw_text: Lastly, may the Philippine Senate remain united in fulfilling its role as the last bastion of democracy. Within these Halls, may truth prevail, and the true voice of the Filipinos be heard. May compassion for others be a steadfast virtue we live by in our words and in our actions, and may we. Your senator-judges, be instruments of Your Grace in the pursuit of peace, justice, and the common good.
blocks.source_text:
Lastly, may the Philippine Senate remain united in fulfilling its role as the last bastion 
of democracy. Within these Halls, may truth prevail, and the true voice of the Filipinos 
be heard. May compassion for others be a steadfast virtue we live by in our words and 
in our actions, and may we. Your senator-judges, be instruments of Your Grace in the 
pursuit of peace, justice, and the common good.

blocks.source_text:
Mr. Lotoc. Sir, ‘yung una po naming ginawa, we tried to invite Princess Maui to shed light 
doon sa sinasabi nilang Romanov. Since hindi ho nag-appear si Princess Maui, wala ho kaming 
basis, so, ki«-classify muna natin ito na non-validated information. Naghanap kami sa open 
source, lyon nga, nakita namin ang unang nagbigkas niyan, si Mayor Baste, so medyo nakita 
namin, teka muna parang baliktad yata ang sitwasyon. Kung kay Mayor Baste nagsimula ang 
Romanov, bakit dito—ito ‘yung tinatanong ni Princess Maui sa Bise Presidente. So, medyo it 
doesn’t make sense. Sir. Kaya wala kaming nu-validate, since hindi «ag-appear si Princess Maui, 
so we classified the information as unvalidated information.

blocks.normalized_text: i will no longer dwell on the operation romanov despite the fact that thank you to senator-judge raffy tulfo who told me about the dates i will not even mention or tackle the conditional threat but i was looking at this nbi letter dated 2 december 2024 marked as annex i this is prosecution s p-iv-5 exhibit invited respondent duterte to appear in cormection with the investigation of the alleged grave threats
blocks.raw_text: I will no longer dwell on the Operation Romanov, despite the fact that—thank you to Senator- Judge Raffy Tulfo, who told me about the dates. I will not even mention or tackle the conditional threat. blocks.source_text:
I will no longer dwell on the Operation Romanov, despite the fact that—thank you to Senator- 
Judge Raffy Tulfo, who told me about the dates. I will not even mention or tackle the conditional 
threat. But I was looking at this NBI letter dated 2 December 2024, marked as Annex I, this is 
prosecution’s P-IV-5 exhibit, invited respondent Duterte to appear in cormection with the 
investigation of the alleged grave threats.

blocks.normalized_text: yes sir una since yung second letter ng vice president through her lawyer may sinasabi doon na may threat sa life six months ago pa so noong tinray try naming z-check iyon your honor ang nakita lang naming relevant information as to the security yung pagbabawas ho ng 75 pnp personnel from bpspg if i m not mistaken and then i think tinransfer hoyata yung mga persoimel na iyon sa manila and then when we checked the record iyon nga based on the coa record meron pang 335 more or less if i m not mistaken na security persormel ang bise presidente
blocks.raw_text: Yes, Sir. Una, since ‘yung second letter ng Vice President through her lawyer, may sinasabi doon na may threat sa life six months ago pa, so noong tinray (try) naming z-check iyon. Your Honor, ang nakita lang naming relevant information as to the security, 'yung pagbabawas ho ng 75 PNP personnel from BPSPG, if I’m not mistaken. And then, I think tinransfer hoyata 'yung mga persoimel na iyon sa Manila. And then when we checked the record, iyon nga, based on the COA record, meron pang 335 more or less, if I’m not mistaken, na security persormel ang Bise Presidente.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Lotoc. Yes, Sir. Una, since ‘yung second letter ng Vice President through her lawyer, may 
sinasabi doon na may threat sa life six months ago pa, so noong tinray (try) naming z-check iyon. 
Your Honor, ang nakita lang naming relevant information as to the security, 'yung pagbabawas 
ho ng 75 PNP personnel from BPSPG, if I’m not mistaken. And then, I think tinransfer hoyata 'yung 
mga persoimel na iyon sa Manila. And then when we checked the record, iyon nga, based on 
the COA record, meron pang 335 more or less, if I’m not mistaken, na security persormel ang 
Bise Presidente.

blocks.normalized_text: i ask this question mr witness and for our people kasi ang hindi na po iyong operation romanov hindi na po itong conditional threat kung hindi ito ba ay nabigyan accorded the same level of attention doon sa mga threats na inyong nakukuha gaya ng binabanggit natin na threat diumano ng ating bise presidente kasi ang tanong hindi rin naman po nagpunta sa inyo ang pangulo o ang speaker of the house and yet you pursued your investigation so in that way paano n yo mnz -explain ito attorney lotoc na na ginawa n yo talaga yung lahat ng inyong magagawa para imbestigahan din iyong threat laban sa pangalawang pangulo
blocks.raw_text: I ask this question, Mr. Witness, and for our people. Kasi ang— hindi na po iyong Operation Romanov, hindi na po itong conditional threat, kung hindi ito ba ay nabigyan—accorded the same level of attention doon sa mga threats na inyong nakukuha gaya ng binabanggit natin na threat diumano ng ating Bise Presidente. Kasi ang tanong, hindi rin naman po nagpunta sa inyo ang Pangulo o ang Speaker of the House, and yet, you pursued your investigation. So, in that way, paano n 'yo mnz'-explain ito Attorney Lotoc na—na ginawa n’yo talaga ‘yung lahat ng inyong magagawa para imbestigahan din iyong threat laban sa Pangalawang Pangulo?
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Villanueva. I ask this question, Mr. Witness, and for our people. Kasi ang— 
hindi na po iyong Operation Romanov, hindi na po itong conditional threat, kung hindi ito ba 
ay nabigyan—accorded the same level of attention doon sa mga threats na inyong nakukuha 
gaya ng binabanggit natin na threat diumano ng ating Bise Presidente. Kasi ang tanong, hindi 
rin naman po nagpunta sa inyo ang Pangulo o ang Speaker of the House, and yet, you pursued 
your investigation. So, in that way, paano n 'yo mnz'-explain ito Attorney Lotoc na—na ginawa 
n’yo talaga ‘yung lahat ng inyong magagawa para imbestigahan din iyong threat laban sa 
Pangalawang Pangulo?

blocks.normalized_text: well in inciting to sedition your honor specifically in the case of people vs nabong it is not necessary m magkaroon ng actual sedition the law also penalizes if the seditious words would encourage people to do illegal acts or to overthrow government in other words if the speeches would stir up the people to go against the government if the speeches would disturb the peace of the community if the speeches would undermine public order public security hindi po necessary na magkaroon ng actual na sedition that s under the law sir
blocks.raw_text: Well, in inciting to sedition. Your Honor, specifically in the case of People vs. Nabong, it is not necessary m magkaroon ng actual sedition. The law also penalizes if the seditious words would encourage people to do illegal acts or to overthrow government. In other words, if the speeches would stir up the people to go against the government, if the speeches would disturb the peace of the community, if the speeches would undermine public order, public security, hindi po necessary na magkaroon ng actual na sedition. That’s under the law. Sir.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Lotoc. Well, in inciting to sedition. Your Honor, specifically in the case of People vs. 
Nabong, it is not necessary m magkaroon ng actual sedition. The law also penalizes if the 
seditious words would encourage people to do illegal acts or to overthrow government. In other 
words, if the speeches would stir up the people to go against the government, if the speeches 
would disturb the peace of the community, if the speeches would undermine public order, public 
security, hindi po necessary na magkaroon ng actual na sedition. That’s under the law. Sir.

blocks.raw_text: Now, before I proceed, gusto kong magbigay ng example para maintindihan ng mga kababayan natin. This is among two professional boxers. Is that a grave threat? Third example, pul is, sabi, “Bubunutan ko ito ng baril. ” Is that a grave threat? Maybe it is just offensive dahil sa profession nila, you should not say that to a fellow police. Now, before I proceed, gusto kong magbigay ng example para maintindihan ng mga 
kababayan natin. Kung magkapitbahay, nagkapikunan doon sa bakod, tapos sabi noong isa, 
“Bobolohin ko ito. ” Is that a grave threat? Second example, dalawang boksingero, nagkainitan, 
“Susuntukin kita." This is among two professional boxers. Is that a grave threat? Third example, 
pul is, sabi, “Bubunutan ko ito ng baril. ” Is that a grave threat? Maybe it is just offensive dahil 
sa profession nila, you should not say that to a fellow police. And my fourth example is a senator, 
pag sinabi niyang, “Bibitbitin kita, kukuwelyuhan kita, palalayasin kita. ” Is that a grave threat?

blocks.normalized_text: iyong sagot sa tanong na iyan iyong sagot po ng prosecution hindi po galing sa opinyon ng aking mga kasama ito po ay isang decision no less than the supreme court of the philippines it just so happened your honors as an answer in answer po sa tanong ni senator pia cayetano may dalawang kaso na po iyong supreme court iyong isa is paera vs people g r no 181626 may 30 2011 sinabi ng supreme court doon wala pong batas na nagre-require na may private complainant ibig sabihin hindi po required na iyong biktima wag-execute ng affidavit upang iyong akusado pupuwedeng wa-found guilty ng grave threats paera v5 people
blocks.raw_text: Iyong sagot sa tanong na iyan, iyong sagot po ng prosecution, hindi po galing sa opinyon ng aking mga kasama. Ito po ay isang decision, no less than the Supreme Court of the Philippines. It just so happened. Your Honors, as an answer—in answer po sa tanong ni Senator Pia Cayetano, may dalawang kaso na po iyong Supreme Court: iyong isa is Paera vs. People—G.R. No. 181626, May 30, 2011. Sinabi ng Supreme Court doon, wala pong batas na nagre-require na may private complainant. Ibig sabihin, hindi po required na iyong biktima wag-execute ng affidavit upang iyong akusado pupuwedeng wa-found guilty ng grave threats— Paera V5. People.
blocks.source_text:
Iyong sagot sa tanong na iyan, iyong sagot po ng prosecution, hindi po galing sa opinyon 
ng aking mga kasama. Ito po ay isang decision, no less than the Supreme Court of the Philippines. 
It just so happened. Your Honors, as an answer—in answer po sa tanong ni Senator Pia Cayetano, 
may dalawang kaso na po iyong Supreme Court: iyong isa is Paera vs. People—G.R. No. 181626, 
May 30, 2011. Sinabi ng Supreme Court doon, wala pong batas na nagre-require na may private 
complainant. Ibig sabihin, hindi po required na iyong biktima wag-execute ng affidavit upang 
iyong akusado pupuwedeng wa-found guilty ng grave threats— Paera V5. People.

blocks.normalized_text: but more than that your honor please may isang kaso pa no less than the supreme court of the philippines sinabi doon hindi po kailangan na iyong recipient mismo nakaramdam ng takot or that sineryoso iyong pagbabanta your honor to repeat ito ang sinabi ng supreme court all that needs to be proven is that they were intended by the accused to have that effect iyong sagot sa tanong hindi po galing sa prosecution hindi po opinyon galing mismo sa supreme court of the philippines your honor
blocks.raw_text: But more than that. Your Honor, please, may isang kaso pa, no less than the Supreme Court of the Philippines, sinabi doon, hindi po kailangan na iyong recipient mismo nakaramdam ng takot or that sineryoso iyong pagbabanta. Your Honor, to repeat, ito ang sinabi ng Supreme Court, “all that needs to be proven is that they were intended by the accused to have that effect.” Iyong sagot sa tanong, hindi po galing sa prosecution, hindi po opinyon, galing mismo sa Supreme Court of the Philippines, Your Honor.
blocks.source_text:
But more than that. Your Honor, please, may isang kaso pa, no less than the Supreme Court 
of the Philippines, sinabi doon, hindi po kailangan na iyong recipient mismo nakaramdam ng 
takot or that sineryoso iyong pagbabanta. Your Honor, to repeat, ito ang sinabi ng Supreme 
Court, “all that needs to be proven is that they were intended by the accused to have that effect.” 
Iyong sagot sa tanong, hindi po galing sa prosecution, hindi po opinyon, galing mismo sa 
Supreme Court of the Philippines, Your Honor.

blocks.normalized_text: if i may add senator-judge pia if i remember my memory correctly there are only certain private crimes that need a private complainant and most of these are personal crimes such as concubinage adultery seduction abduction rape and acts of lasciviousness although these crimes are all entitled people of the philippines versus the accused without a private complainant these crimes cannot be filed by the prosecutor in court without the cooperation or affidavit of the private complainant
blocks.raw_text: If I may add, Senator-Judge Pia, if I remember my memory correctly, there are only certain private crimes that need a private complainant, and most of these are personal crimes such as concubinage, adultery, seduction, abduction, rape, and acts of lasciviousness. Although these Crimes are all entitled. People of the Philippines versus the accused, without a private complainant, these crimes cannot be filed by the prosecutor in court without the cooperation or affidavit of the private complainant.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer. If I may add, Senator-Judge Pia, if I remember my memory correctly, 
there are only certain private crimes that need a private complainant, and most of these are personal 
crimes such as concubinage, adultery, seduction, abduction, rape, and acts of lasciviousness. 
Although these Crimes are all entitled. People of the Philippines versus the accused, without a 
private complainant, these crimes cannot be filed by the prosecutor in court without the 
cooperation or affidavit of the private complainant.

blocks.normalized_text: actually hang beses ko po binasa iyong affidavit and what i see is there was a focus on the october 18 but on all the events on november 22 and 23 the detailed events leading to the emotional outburst did not seem complete and that s what i m trying to elicit from attorney lotoc our witness because when we say that we had considered the bigger picture and all the facts and circumstances leading to the cases of grave threat and inciting to sedition that were recommended we want to understand the whole process and the factual context in which it happened because if you are to take snapshots of events and focus on october 18 and the press conference it can shock any person however if you see as well the events leading to the emotions and the detention of the chief of staff and the vice president herself iba pa iyong kasong ito iba pa iyong na-detina iba pa iyong isinampang kaso at pinagsarahan ng pinto ng ambulansiya
blocks.raw_text: Actually, Hang beses ko po binasa iyong affidavit. And what I see is, there was a focus on the October 18, but on all the events on November 22 and 23, the detailed events leading to the emotional outburst did not seem complete. And that’s what I’m trying to elicit from Attorney Lotoc, our witness, because when we say that we had considered the bigger picture, and all the facts and circumstances leading to the cases of grave threat and inciting to sedition that were recommended. We want to understand the whole process and the factual context in which it happened. Because if you are to take snapshots of events and focus on October 18 and the press conference, it can shock any person. However, if you see as well the events leading to the emotions and the detention of the chief of staff and the Vice President herself, iba pa iyong kasong ito, iba pa iyong na-detina, iba pa iyong isinampang kaso, at pinagsarahan ng pinto ng ambulansiya,
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Legarda. Actually, Hang beses ko po binasa iyong affidavit. And what I see 
is, there was a focus on the October 18, but on all the events on November 22 and 23, the detailed 
events leading to the emotional outburst did not seem complete. And that’s what I’m trying to elicit 
from Attorney Lotoc, our witness, because when we say that we had considered the bigger picture, 
and all the facts and circumstances leading to the cases of grave threat and inciting to sedition 
that were recommended. We want to understand the whole process and the factual context in which 
it happened. Because if you are to take snapshots of events and focus on October 18 and the press 
conference, it can shock any person. However, if you see as well the events leading to the emotions 
and the detention of the chief of staff and the Vice President herself, iba pa iyong kasong ito, iba 
pa iyong na-detina, iba pa iyong isinampang kaso, at pinagsarahan ng pinto ng ambulansiya,

blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Villanueva. Yes. Again, let me ask the witness just to clarify the factual basis 
for the National Bureau of Investigation’s possible inciting to sedition. We recognize, Mr. Witness, 
your strong criticism of the President may fall within the realm of what we call protected political 
speech and it’s really a balance and somehow iyon iyong nagpapa-confused pa minsan, kailan 
nagpo-iaW into grave threats, kailan nagpo-iaW into inciting to sedition.

blocks.normalized_text: yes your honor well una kinonsider namin sino iyong utterer so una iyong utterer the vice president the second highest official of the land then pangalawa kinonsider namin ano ba iyong mga im-utter ng vice president and then pangatlo ano ba iyong surrounding circumstances na like naging reaction ng recipient ng utterances o threats nakita ho namin na kung pwede palang gawin ito ng isa sa pinakamataas na posisyon ng pilipinas at hindi natin ito m-address hindi natin ire-regulate pwede na po pala itong gawin ng kahit na sino remember your honor vice president is kinokonsider natin na model bilang public official so tinitingnan po natin siya kung ano iyong actuations niya sa public kung ano iyong pakikipagrelasyon niya sa ibang public officials
blocks.raw_text: Yes, Your Honor. Well, una, kinonsider namin sino iyong utterer. So, una, iyong utterer, the Vice President, the second highest official of the land. Then, pangalawa, kinonsider namin, ano ba iyong mga im-utter ng Vice President. And then, pangatlo, ano ba iyong surrounding circumstances na, like, naging reaction ng recipient ng utterances o threats. Nakita ho namin na kung pwede palang gawin ito ng isa sa pinakamataas na posisyon ng Pilipinas at hindi natin ito m-address, hindi natin ire-regulate, pwede na po pala itong gawin ng kahit na sino. Remember, Your Honor, Vice President is kinokonsider natin na model bilang public official. So, tinitingnan po natin siya kung ano iyong actuations niya sa public, kung ano iyong pakikipagrelasyon niya sa ibang public officials.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Lotoc. Yes, Your Honor. Well, una, kinonsider namin sino iyong utterer. So, una, iyong 
utterer, the Vice President, the second highest official of the land. Then, pangalawa, kinonsider 
namin, ano ba iyong mga im-utter ng Vice President. And then, pangatlo, ano ba iyong 
surrounding circumstances na, like, naging reaction ng recipient ng utterances o threats. Nakita 
ho namin na kung pwede palang gawin ito ng isa sa pinakamataas na posisyon ng Pilipinas 
at hindi natin ito m-address, hindi natin ire-regulate, pwede na po pala itong gawin ng kahit 
na sino. Remember, Your Honor, Vice President is kinokonsider natin na model bilang public 
official. So, tinitingnan po natin siya kung ano iyong actuations niya sa public, kung ano iyong 
pakikipagrelasyon niya sa ibang public officials.

blocks.normalized_text: so again your honor noong makita ho namin iyan may effect po sa mga tao kung ang vice president kayang gawin iyan at wala namang nangyari gawin na rin natin iyong iba
blocks.raw_text: So, again, Your Honor, noong makita ho namin iyan, may effect po sa mga tao. Kung ang Vice President kayang gawin iyan, at wala namang nangyari, gawin na rin natin, iyong iba.
blocks.source_text:
So, again, Your Honor, noong makita ho namin iyan, may effect po sa mga tao. Kung ang 
Vice President kayang gawin iyan, at wala namang nangyari, gawin na rin natin, iyong iba.

Your Honor, I invoke Rule 130, Section 53 of the Rules of Court, if I may read, “opinion of ordinary witnesses,” general rule, not allowed, but I think if we read the exceptions. if I may read, “opinion of ordinary witnesses,” general rule, not allowed, but I think if we read the 
exceptions. Your Honor, it says “the witness may testify,” however, “on his impressions of the 
emotion, behavior, condition, or appearance of a person.” Section 53, Rule 130, specifically allows

she uttered those confirmation threats. It’s in the Rules, Your Honor.

blocks.normalized_text: doon sa november 26 your honor i think ang isa sa issue niya na may threat sa security is iyong paglipat ng vpspg from attached unit ng afp na maging under siya ng presidential security command iyon po iyong narinig namin pero dito sa 75 personnel insofar as i m concerned wala ho akong nakita sa reports sa news sa social media na nagreklamo siya na nawala iyong 75 pero baka mali ho ako baka meron lang akong hindi nakita pero as far as i m concerned wala ho akong nakita sa news na nagreklamo siya doon sa 75 transfer ng pnp personnel fi om vpspg na dinala doon sa manila your honor
blocks.raw_text: Doon sa November 26, Your Honor, I think, ang isa sa issue niya na may threat sa security, is iyong paglipat ng VPSPG from attached unit ng AFP na maging under siya ng Presidential Security Command. Iyon po iyong narinig namin. Pero dito sa 75 personnel, insofar as I’m concerned, wala ho akong nakita sa reports, sa news, sa social media na nagreklamo siya na nawala iyong 75. Pero baka mali ho ako, baka meron lang akong hindi nakita. Pero, as far as I’m concerned, wala ho akong nakita sa news na nagreklamo siya doon sa 75 transfer ng PNP personnel fi'om VPSPG na dinala doon sa Manila, Your Honor.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Lotoc. Doon sa November 26, Your Honor, I think, ang isa sa issue niya na may threat 
sa security, is iyong paglipat ng VPSPG from attached unit ng AFP na maging under siya ng 
Presidential Security Command. Iyon po iyong narinig namin. Pero dito sa 75 personnel, insofar 
as I’m concerned, wala ho akong nakita sa reports, sa news, sa social media na nagreklamo 
siya na nawala iyong 75. Pero baka mali ho ako, baka meron lang akong hindi nakita. Pero, 
as far as I’m concerned, wala ho akong nakita sa news na nagreklamo siya doon sa 75 transfer 
ng PNP personnel fi'om VPSPG na dinala doon sa Manila, Your Honor.

blocks.source_text:
An admission against interest under the rules is simply an exception to the hearsay rule, where 
a person is about to die or is unavailable to testify, his statements against his or her interest may 
be admitted, testified to by another person, not the way we seem to be giving admission against 
interest na parang umamin na, dahil labag sa interes niya iyon. The rules are specific, and the 
purpose also is specific. It is an exception to the hearsay rule. If there are no other points or 
questions to be raised, any of the senators-judges, attorney—talagang photo finish.

Staff of the Office of the Vice President, covered only the previous and the current day’s 
proceedings, while Capt. Belinda Bello, head of the Legislative Security Bureau of the House 
of Representatives (HOR), had not been subpoenaed but was merely scheduled to testify 
the following day.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a sought clarification on whether the oral arguments on the requests for subpoenas covering bank records bureau of internal revenue bir records and anti-money laundering council amlc documents would proceed the following day presiding officer escudero confirmed that they would and informed the body that the parties memoranda had already been submitted and distributed to the senator-judges
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) sought clarification on whether the oral arguments on the requests for subpoenas covering bank records. Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) records, and Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) documents would proceed the following day. Presiding Officer Escudero confirmed that they would and informed the Body that the parties’ memoranda had already been submitted and distributed to the senator-judges.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) sought clarification on whether the oral arguments on the requests 
for subpoenas covering bank records. Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) records, and Anti-Money 
Laundering Council (AMLC) documents would proceed the following day. Presiding Officer 
Escudero confirmed that they would and informed the Body that the parties’ memoranda had 
already been submitted and distributed to the senator-judges.

blocks.normalized_text: noting that the memoranda comprised approximately 60 pages senator-judge cayetano a inquired whether the court would rule immediately after the oral arguments or deliberate further before rendering its decision on monday presiding officer escudero replied that the court intended to decide the matter the following day to give the parties ample time to examine records produced in case the subpoenas were granted adding that the requested documents were in relation to article ii the hearing of which would only begin in august while presiding officer escudero confirmed that under the current schedule it was the last article to be heard the prosecution has the discretion to alter the sequence of its presentation
blocks.raw_text: Noting that the memoranda comprised approximately 60 pages, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) inquired whether the Court would rule immediately after the oral arguments or deliberate further before rendering its decision on Monday. Presiding Officer Escudero replied that the Court intended to decide the matter the following day to give the parties ample time to examine records produced in case the subpoenas were granted, adding that the requested documents were in relation to Article II, the hearing of which would only begin in August.While Presiding Officer Escudero confirmed that under the current schedule, it was the last article to be heard, the Prosecution has the discretion to alter the sequence of its presentation.
blocks.source_text:
Noting that the memoranda comprised approximately 60 pages, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) 
inquired whether the Court would rule immediately after the oral arguments or deliberate further 
before rendering its decision on Monday. Presiding Officer Escudero replied that the Court intended 
to decide the matter the following day to give the parties ample time to examine records produced 
in case the subpoenas were granted, adding that the requested documents were in relation to Article 
II, the hearing of which would only begin in August.While Presiding Officer Escudero confirmed 
that under the current schedule, it was the last article to be heard, the Prosecution has the discretion 
to alter the sequence of its presentation.

blocks.normalized_text: clarifying that he was not proposing to defer the oral arguments senator-judge cayetano a suggested that the senator-judges first hold a caucus and deliberate before voting on the requests he noted that a similar procedure had been followed in a previous impeachment trial and that the issues involving the amlc and bir records could establish important precedents presiding officer escudero then referred the proposal to convene a caucus to senate president gatchalian
blocks.raw_text: Clarifying that he was not proposing to defer the oral arguments, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) suggested that the senator-judges first hold a caucus and deliberate before voting on the requests. He noted that a similar procedure had been followed in a previous impeachment trial and that the issues involving the AMLC and BIR records could establish important precedents. Presiding Officer Escudero then referred the proposal to convene a caucus to Senate President Gatchalian.
blocks.source_text:
Clarifying that he was not proposing to defer the oral arguments, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) 
suggested that the senator-judges first hold a caucus and deliberate before voting on the 
requests. He noted that a similar procedure had been followed in a previous impeachment trial 
and that the issues involving the AMLC and BIR records could establish important precedents. 
Presiding Officer Escudero then referred the proposal to convene a caucus to Senate President 
Gatchalian.

blocks.raw_text: Senate President Gatchalian agreed with Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) that the parties’ memoranda were lengthy and complicated, requiring review by the senator-judges and their legal teams. He initially proposed holding a caucus at 10:00 a.m. the following day.
blocks.source_text:
Senate President Gatchalian agreed with Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) that the parties’ 
memoranda were lengthy and complicated, requiring review by the senator-judges and their legal 
teams. He initially proposed holding a caucus at 10:00 a.m. the following day.

blocks.normalized_text: upon senator-judge cayetano a s suggestion however the court agreed to convene the caucus after the oral arguments during a break in the proceedings to allow the senator-judges to first hear the parties presentations before deliberating
blocks.raw_text: Upon Senator-Judge Cayetano (A)’s suggestion, however, the Court agreed to convene the caucus after the oral arguments, during a break in the proceedings, to allow the senator-judges to first hear the parties’ presentations before deliberating.
blocks.source_text:
Upon Senator-Judge Cayetano (A)’s suggestion, however, the Court agreed to convene the 
caucus after the oral arguments, during a break in the proceedings, to allow the senator-judges to 
first hear the parties’ presentations before deliberating.

blocks.normalized_text: attorney loma p kapunan counsel for the prosecution manifested that the prosecution would no longer present attorney zuleika lopez and capt belinda bello as witnesses she explained that the prosecution considered their testimonies unnecessary in light of admissions made by the defense after four trial days of testimony the presentation of 19 documentary exhibits authenticated by nbi agent john mark calilung and 45 documentary exhibits authenticated by atty lotoc together with the questions of the senator-judges
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Loma P. Kapunan, Counsel for the Prosecution, manifested that the Prosecution would no longer present Attorney Zuleika Lopez and Capt. Belinda Bello as witnesses. She explained that the Prosecution considered their testimonies unnecessary in light of admissions made by the Defense, after four trial days of testimony, the presentation of 19 documentary exhibits authenticated by NBI Agent John Mark Calilung and 45 documentary exhibits authenticated by Atty. Lotoc, together with the questions of the senator-judges.
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Loma P. Kapunan, Counsel for the Prosecution, manifested that the Prosecution 
would no longer present Attorney Zuleika Lopez and Capt. Belinda Bello as witnesses. She 
explained that the Prosecution considered their testimonies unnecessary in light of admissions made 
by the Defense, after four trial days of testimony, the presentation of 19 documentary exhibits 
authenticated by NBI Agent John Mark Calilung and 45 documentary exhibits authenticated by 
Atty. Lotoc, together with the questions of the senator-judges.

blocks.normalized_text: she further explained that attorney lopez s proposed testimony had become unnecessary because the defense had effectively admitted the vice president s statements the presence of the media and the authenticity of the video including by relying on the same video as evidence she emphasized that the impeachment court s role is not to determine criminal liability for grave threats or inciting to sedition but to decide whether the respondent s admitted acts constitute impeachable offenses under the constitution she further stated that captain bello s testimony would have been merely corroborative limited to the detention transfer order and its relation to the vice president s november 23 2024 press conference
blocks.raw_text: She further explained that Attorney Lopez’s proposed testimony had become unnecessary because the Defense had effectively admitted the Vice President’s statements, the presence of the media, and the authenticity of the video, including by relying on the same video as evidence. She emphasized that the Impeachment Court’s role is not to determine criminal liability for Grave Threats or Inciting to Sedition, but to decide whether the Respondent’s admitted acts constitute impeachable offenses under the Constitution. She further stated that Captain Bello’s testimony would have been merely corroborative, limited to the detention transfer order and its relation to the Vice President’s November 23, 2024 press conference.
blocks.source_text:
She further explained that Attorney Lopez’s proposed testimony had become unnecessary 
because the Defense had effectively admitted the Vice President’s statements, the presence of the 
media, and the authenticity of the video, including by relying on the same video as evidence. She 
emphasized that the Impeachment Court’s role is not to determine criminal liability for Grave 
Threats or Inciting to Sedition, but to decide whether the Respondent’s admitted acts constitute 
impeachable offenses under the Constitution. She further stated that Captain Bello’s testimony 
would have been merely corroborative, limited to the detention transfer order and its relation to the 
Vice President’s November 23, 2024 press conference.

blocks.normalized_text: she added that dispensing with the two witnesses would promote the respondent s right to a speedy trial and expressed hope that the defense would adopt the same approach

blocks.normalized_text: senate president gatchalian expressed gratitude to the prosecution for dispensing with the presentation of two witnesses noting that it would help expedite the proceedings he observed that the pre-trial order listed 57 prosecution witnesses and 45 defense witnesses or 102 witnesses in total and estimated that at the current pace of two hearing days per witness the trial could last about 17 months he thus encouraged both parties to continue adopting measures that would shorten the proceedings
blocks.raw_text: Senate President Gatchalian expressed gratitude to the Prosecution for dispensing with the presentation of two witnesses, noting that it would help expedite the proceedings. He observed that the pre-trial order listed 57 prosecution witnesses and 45 defense witnesses, or 102 witnesses in total, and estimated that, at the current pace of two hearing days per witness, the trial could last about 17 months. He thus encouraged both parties to continue adopting measures that would shorten the proceedings.
blocks.source_text:
Senate President Gatchalian expressed gratitude to the Prosecution for dispensing with 
the presentation of two witnesses, noting that it would help expedite the proceedings. He observed 
that the pre-trial order listed 57 prosecution witnesses and 45 defense witnesses, or 102 witnesses 
in total, and estimated that, at the current pace of two hearing days per witness, the trial could last 
about 17 months. He thus encouraged both parties to continue adopting measures that would 
shorten the proceedings.

blocks.normalized_text: presiding officer escudero thanked the prosecution for dispensing with the two witnesses and announced that the july 15 2026 hearing would instead be devoted to the oral arguments on the prosecution s motions for subpoenas covering bank amlc and bir records with nbi director melvin matibag s testimony reset to july 20 2026 he directed that the motions be argued jointly in accordance with the parties consolidated memoranda he initially allotted 10 minutes per side for the principal arguments and three minutes each for rebuttal and surrebuttal and informed the body that the senator-judges would deliberate in caucus before the court ruled on the motions
blocks.raw_text: Presiding Officer Escudero thanked the Prosecution for dispensing with the two witnesses and announced that the July 15, 2026 hearing would instead be devoted to the oral arguments on the Prosecution’s motions for subpoenas covering bank, AMLC, and BIR records. With NBI Director Melvin Matibag’s testimony reset to July 20, 2026, he directed that the motions be argued jointly in accordance with the parties’ consolidated memoranda. He initially allotted 10 minutes per side for the principal arguments and three minutes each for rebuttal and surrebuttal, and informed the Body that the senator-judges would deliberate in caucus before the Court ruled on the motions.
blocks.source_text:
Presiding Officer Escudero thanked the Prosecution for dispensing with the two witnesses 
and announced that the July 15, 2026 hearing would instead be devoted to the oral arguments 
on the Prosecution’s motions for subpoenas covering bank, AMLC, and BIR records. With NBI 
Director Melvin Matibag’s testimony reset to July 20, 2026, he directed that the motions be 
argued jointly in accordance with the parties’ consolidated memoranda. He initially allotted 
10 minutes per side for the principal arguments and three minutes each for rebuttal and 
surrebuttal, and informed the Body that the senator-judges would deliberate in caucus before 
the Court ruled on the motions.

He added that more comprehensive presentations would better assist both the Court and the public and could reduce the need for questions.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) proposed extending the time for oral arguments to 15 to 20 
minutes per side, emphasizing the importance of the subpoena motions and the need to distinguish 
legitimate requests for evidence from a fishing expedition. He added that more comprehensive 
presentations would better assist both the Court and the public and could reduce the need for 
questions.

blocks.raw_text: Upon the parties’ manifestation, Presiding Officer Escudero approved 15 minutes per side for the principal arguments and 10 minutes each for rebuttal and surrebuttal. He likewise reminded the senator-judges that, under the Rules, each may speak for up to 10 minutes, subject to an aggregate limit of one hour per side during plenary debate, if necessary, and urged the parties to maximize their allotted time.
blocks.source_text:
Upon the parties’ manifestation, Presiding Officer Escudero approved 15 minutes per side for 
the principal arguments and 10 minutes each for rebuttal and surrebuttal. He likewise reminded the 
senator-judges that, under the Rules, each may speak for up to 10 minutes, subject to an aggregate 
limit of one hour per side during plenary debate, if necessary, and urged the parties to maximize 
their allotted time.

blocks.normalized_text: if the court failed to reach a consensus in caucus presiding officer escudero clarified that the two-minute limit applied only to clarificatory questions during evidentiary proceedings clarifying that in this case both sides will be given enough time to present their arguments in chief and on rebuttal the senators will have the opportunity to listen and thereafter go into caucus failing a consensus in caucus a debate may ensue for a maximum of two hours - one hour for each side he also ruled that the amlc and bir subpoena motions would be heard jointly because the parties had submitted consolidated memoranda senator-judge sotto then suggested deferring any adjustment of the senator-judges speaking time until after the caucus to which the presiding officer agreed
blocks.raw_text: if the Court failed to reach a consensus in caucus. Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the two-minute limit applied only to clarificatory questions during evidentiary proceedings, clarifying that in this case, both sides will be given enough time to present their arguments in chief and on rebuttal. The Senators will have the opportunity to listen and thereafter go into caucus. Failing a consensus in caucus, a debate may ensue for a maximum of two hours - one hour for each side. He also ruled that the AMLC and BIR subpoena motions would be heard jointly because the parties had submitted consolidated memoranda. Senator-Judge Sotto then suggested deferring any adjustment of the senator-judges’ speaking time until after the caucus, to which the Presiding Officer agreed.
blocks.source_text:
if the Court failed to reach a consensus in caucus. Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the 
two-minute limit applied only to clarificatory questions during evidentiary proceedings, clarifying that 
in this case, both sides will be given enough time to present their arguments in chief and on rebuttal. 
The Senators will have the opportunity to listen and thereafter go into caucus. Failing a consensus 
in caucus, a debate may ensue for a maximum of two hours - one hour for each side. He also 
ruled that the AMLC and BIR subpoena motions would be heard jointly because the parties had 
submitted consolidated memoranda. Senator-Judge Sotto then suggested deferring any adjustment 
of the senator-judges’ speaking time until after the caucus, to which the Presiding Officer agreed.

blocks.raw_text: Mas was not dismissed on the merits. He explained that the Regional Trial Court (RTC) dismissed the case on June 24 after granting a Motion to Quash based on unlawful arrest, resulting in the court’s lack of jurisdiction over the accused, and emphasized that the dismissal was purely procedural and did not resolve the Inciting to Sedition charge on the merits.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Pangilinan clarified for the record that People v. Mas was not dismissed on 
the merits. He explained that the Regional Trial Court (RTC) dismissed the case on June 24 after 
granting a Motion to Quash based on unlawful arrest, resulting in the court’s lack of jurisdiction over 
the accused, and emphasized that the dismissal was purely procedural and did not resolve the 
Inciting to Sedition charge on the merits.
